decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly placing powerful creatures on the battlefield by discarding cards and utilizing the graveyard. It seeks to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and take control of the game early on. The goal is to utilize cards like Burning Inquiry and Faithless Looting to quickly bring out creatures like Vengevine and Hollow One, while also utilizing Lightning Bolt for removal. The deck also includes synergies with Detective's Phoenix and Marauding Mako, making it a fast-paced and formidable aggro deck to play in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and mana dorks to cast powerful storm spells like Past in Flames and Ral, Monsoon Mage. It aims to set up explosive turns by chaining together spells, leading to a storm count high enough to win the game with spells like Wish or Artist's Talent. The deck also includes cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Hex Magic to protect its combo pieces. Overall, the key concept is to assemble a critical mass of spells to overwhelm the opponent in a single turn.

The main focus of this aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu to apply pressure, backed up by removal spells like Fatal Push and Stubborn Denial, the deck aims to control the board and deliver a steady stream of damage to the opponent. Leyline of the Guildpact and Quantum Riddler provide additional support to the deck's strategy, while cards like Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger offer additional disruptive elements to keep the opponent on their toes.

This deck focuses on assembling the Broodscale Bloodchief combo to generate massive amounts of mana and overwhelm the opponent with Eldrazi creatures. The key is to use cards like Urza's Saga and Ancient Stirrings to find the combo pieces quickly while controlling the board with removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command. The deck also utilizes lands like Eldrazi Temple and Grove of the Burnwillows to ramp up mana production, allowing for explosive plays with Emrakul, the Promised End. Overall, this deck aims to create a powerful board presence and win the game through a devastating combo.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Allosaurus Rider and Neoform/Eldritch Evolution to quickly bring out powerful creatures such as Griselbrand, Atraxa, and Ghalta. The deck includes various ways to protect the combo with cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer, while also having options for additional ramp and card draw. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with large creatures that can quickly close out the game, while also having ways to disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures, utilizing cards such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Seasoned Pyromancer for additional offense and board control. Other cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide utility and support for the aggro strategy. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while efficiently managing resources to secure victory in a fast-paced Modern 1 vs 1 match.

The key concept of this deck is to assemble a fast and aggressive Eldrazi aggro strategy that utilizes cards like Eldrazi Temple and Cavern of Souls to summon powerful Eldrazi creatures quickly. The deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer, while also overwhelming them with a swarm of Eldrazi creatures such as Glaring Fleshraker and Devourer of Destiny. Ugin, Eye of the Storms serves as a powerful finisher, while supporting cards like Wastes and Unclaimed Territory help to ensure consistent mana production.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using the Urza lands to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Ulamog. It also includes powerful control elements like Karn, the Great Creator and Disruptor Flute to disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck aims to lock down the game with cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void while controlling the board with removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command. Overall, the deck aims to quickly ramp up into powerful threats while controlling the game and disrupting opponents' plans.

This deck focuses on utilizing various mill effects to force the opponent to draw out their entire deck, winning the game by decking them out completely. Cards like Ruin Crab, Hedron Crab, and Archive Trap help mill cards from the opponent's library quickly. Other cards like Tasha's Hideous Laughter and Fractured Sanity also contribute to this strategy. The deck also includes disruption and removal spells like Drown in the Loch and Fatal Push to control the board and protect the combo. The key to winning with this deck is efficient milling and maintaining control of the game.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal, and hand disruption. By controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's resources, the deck aims to establish dominance and eventually win the game with powerful threats like Tasigur, the Golden Fang and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The key strategy is to maintain control of the tempo of the game, using a mix of reactive and proactive spells to stay ahead of the opponent and secure victory through a mix of card advantage and threat assessment.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's plays through counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. With a mix of permission spells like Counterspell and Force of Negation, along with removal like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict, this deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot while generating card advantage through cards like Snapcaster Mage and Cling to Dust. The deck also includes some versatile spells like Consult the Star Charts and Subtlety to help with card selection and board control.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and generating value through various synergies. By combining aggressive creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary with powerful artifacts like Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creature damage and disruption. Cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager provide additional versatility and resilience, ensuring that the deck can adapt to different matchups and strategies while maintaining a consistent pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on filling up the graveyard with creatures using cards like Street Wraith and Shardless Agent, then casting Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield. This combo can overwhelm the opponent with a sudden influx of creatures, especially powerful ones like Overlord of the Balemurk. The deck also includes disruption elements like Force of Negation and Subtlety to protect the combo and control the board. Overall, the strategy revolves around leveraging the graveyard as a resource to win the game in a burst of powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage with cards like Narset, Teferi, and Orim's Chant. It utilizes a companion creature, Kaheera, for additional value. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up resources until it can safely resolve powerful threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Monumental Henge. With a mix of control elements and win conditions, this UW Control deck can grind out victories in a slower, more methodical manner.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands to generate a large amount of mana to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and game-changing spells. It utilizes control elements such as Disruptor Flute, Trinisphere, and Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect its own win conditions. Karn, the Great Creator is a key card in the deck, allowing access to artifacts in the sideboard to deal with different matchups. Overall, the strategy revolves around ramping up mana quickly, controlling the board, and deploying massive threats to overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly cycling through cards and disrupting opponent's strategy with cards like Endurance and Subtlety, while setting up for a powerful board wipe and graveyard recursion with Living End. The key strategy is to control the game until you can cast Living End and bring back powerful creatures like Generous Ent and Oliphaunt. This deck relies on efficient card draw, control, and creature recursion to overpower opponents and win through a powerful combo finish.
